---
title: "Debug Math Table"
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    mainfont: "NotoSerifCJKsc-Regular.otf"
    mainfontoptions:
      - "Path=assets/fonts/"
      - "BoldFont=NotoSerifCJKsc-Bold.otf"
    sansfont: "NotoSansCJKsc-Regular.otf"
    sansfontoptions:
      - "Path=assets/fonts/"
      - "BoldFont=NotoSansCJKsc-Bold.otf"
    monofont: "MapleMono-NF-CN-Regular.ttf"
    monofontoptions:
      - "Path=assets/fonts/"
      - "BoldFont=MapleMono-NF-CN-Bold.ttf"
      - "ItalicFont=MapleMono-NF-CN-Italic.ttf"
      - "BoldItalicFont=MapleMono-NF-CN-BoldItalic.ttf"
    mathfont: "latinmodern-math.otf"
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
include-in-header:
  text: |
    \addtokomafont{disposition}{\rmfamily\bfseries}
---

# Inline Math in Paragraph

This variant is expected to fail in this repository toolchain:
`\(\sqrt{n}\)`.

This variant is the safe form to use:
$\sqrt{n}$.

# Display Math

$$
t \approx \text{Sharpe}_{\text{daily}} \sqrt{n}
$$

# Markdown Table with Plain Text Header

| label | value |
| ----- | ----- |
| dollar math in cell | $\sqrt{n}$ |

# Markdown Table with Plain-Text Header for PDF Safety

| Critical value for sqrt(n) | value |
| -------------------------- | ----- |
| test                       | 2.326 |

# Markdown Table with Plain-Text Header but Math in Sentence Below

| label | value |
| ----- | ----- |
| threshold | 2.326 |

If daily Sharpe times $\sqrt{n}$ exceeds 2.326, this sentence should compile.

# Conclusion

In this repository, Quarto to XeLaTeX currently handles `$...$` reliably for
inline math, but `\(...\)` is not being converted safely and should be avoided
in `.qmd` source intended for PDF output.
